text.title: Alert Dedup Playbook Failure Modes
text.summary: Six failure modes that turn dedup into a new incident. Each has a detector and a repair.

failure-mode.description: Silenced alerts forgotten; expired silences re-page noisily without anyone noticing the recurrence.
failure-mode.detector: silenced alert re-fires within 7 days of expiry AND no acknowledgement OR remediation logged in incident system
failure-mode.repair: Add automation: 7-day-before-expiry notification to the team. Pair silence with an incident ticket; ticket review at expiry checks for root-cause fix.

failure-mode.description: Fingerprint collision: two unrelated alerts share the same fingerprint and collapse to one incident, hiding the second alert entirely.
failure-mode.detector: two distinct PromQL / Datadog rules write the same fingerprint label set AND incident count drops sharply for the second rule
failure-mode.repair: Run fingerprint-validate.py to detect collisions before merging routing changes. Add a label specific to one of the rules (e.g. `alert_class`). Re-run audit.

failure-mode.description: On-call burnout: pages per week per rotation > 15 sustained; team starts ignoring pages.
failure-mode.detector: time-to-acknowledge p95 > 30 min AND pages/week/rotation > SLO for 3 consecutive weeks
failure-mode.repair: Emergency dedup sprint: pull top-10 alert families into a working session. Silence with 14-day expiry while runbook + fingerprint work happens. Re-evaluate SLO target.

failure-mode.description: Page-routed alert with no runbook; on-call freelances during the incident.
failure-mode.detector: alert-routing.yaml entries with route=page AND runbook_url empty OR pointing to a 404
failure-mode.repair: Block PR merge to alert-routing.yaml when route=page and runbook_url is missing. Backfill runbooks for existing entries during quarterly review.

failure-mode.description: Routing changes shipped without audit; team silenced an alert that was the canary for a real failure.
failure-mode.detector: routing change commit message lacks reference to audit-report.md OR audit-report period predates commit by > 30 days
failure-mode.repair: CI gate: routing PRs must reference a recent audit-report.md entry. Reject otherwise. Quarterly audit cadence is non-negotiable.

failure-mode.description: SLO trend not reviewed quarterly; team ships dedup work but the metric never improves.
failure-mode.detector: 4-week rolling pages/week/rotation has no meeting attribution OR retro entries reference the SLO trend less than once per quarter
failure-mode.repair: Schedule a quarterly review with calendar block. Treat the SLO trend as a first-class engineering metric. Discuss in engineering all-hands.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
Human checkpoint
section.p: Routing changes require team review (PR + 1 approver minimum). Quarterly audit-report is read in a dedicated meeting; decisions are made collaboratively. Silences require a team-visible record, not a one-person silent click in the UI.
